channel.title: Yee Jian's Blog
channel.description: Recent content on Yee Jian's Blog

item.title: Linux wired earphones white noise solution
How to set up printer in NUS Cinnamon College on Linux
item.description: A step-by-step guide to setting up printer using CUPS and its web interface in Cinnamon College.

item.title: SSH Jump
item.description: How to SSH Jump when you are outside the NUS Network.

item.title: Creating My First Pull Request
item.description: How I contributed the first time to GitLab, and what it meant to me.

item.title: Lessons learned writing the Source Stepper - Part 1
item.description: In this part, I will talk about what is the Source Language, what is the Source Stepper, and some prior knowledge to understanding how the Source Stepper Works.

item.title: CS2103 Software Engineering in Neovim - A Post Reflection
item.description: The lessons I learned after using both NeoVim and IntelliJ.

item.title: CS2103 Software Engineering in Neovim
item.description: My (old) setup trying out software engineering in Java on an editor, rather than IDE. Because why not?

item.title: About me
item.description: Resume please? Here you go! :) Who are you? I am Yee Jian, a second year student in National University of Singapore. Brave enough, I am studying a Double Degree in Computer Science and Mathematics. Challenging problems are the doses of adrenaline rush I can never get enough. You can check out the projects I have done here. I will be graduating in May 2023. Currently actively looking for any software engineering or research internship opportunities.

item.title: Projects
item.description: Source Stepper Try out the substituter! A lambda-calculus based interpreter for the Source language, a sublanguage of JavaScript, written in TypeScript. This interpreter steps through and records each reduction step of the program. It can be used for the first 2 chapters of the source language (Source §1, Source §2) as state changes are not allowed in these languages. Idempotency of pure functions ensures the lambda-calculus based reduction rules evaluate the program correctly.
